title: artocarpus lowii
intro: Artocarpus lowii, also known as the Kamang tree, is a species of plant in the Moraceae family native to the tropical regions of Borneo. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains flavonoids and tannins that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Artocarpus lowii include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Traditionally, the bark of the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be processed into a tincture or dried to make a powder for herbal remedies.
